threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can always tell by how you pronounce 'Mare Street'—the locals say it like 'Mare' not 'Mary.' It's a dead giveaway.
Saw a guy on the 36 coming back from a late shift—he’s fallen asleep in the corner with a takeaway box still in his hand. It was half-open, chips everywhere.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a guy juggling fireballs outside the U-Bahn station while a couple tried to sell handmade bracelets—only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how on U-Bahn platforms, people just chill on the stairs instead of moving to the side? It's like a local culture of standing still.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here when they can spot a tourist a mile away, especially when they’re staring at their phones near a food truck.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a glimpse of someone in a costume trying to fit through the back door on the 47. The driver was just shaking his head, like this happens daily.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí. But the real charm is hidden in the local bars, where the best tapa secrets live.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You’ll see them staring bewildered at the tram map, holding it upside down while juggling a bag of "patatas bravas".

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 'Overground' is a part of the Underground network. Welcome to the maze of transport, mate.
Saw a bloke playing a ukulele on the 68 last night. He had more fans than the late-night DJ at The Lounge. Only in Dulwich, eh?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a guy serenading a bus stop with an accordion while waiting for the 167, I knew I’d found my home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a Turkish market turning into an outdoor dance party on a random Friday afternoon. It's a vibe!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they know which BART entrances to avoid after a breakdown—just watch the flustered newcomers trying to navigate.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a bus at 3rd & Market today, a pigeon got on, strutted down the aisle, and everyone just let it be. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the best tapas are in La Rambla. They miss the hidden gems in Gràcia where the locals actually eat, like the tiny bar on Carrer de l'Or.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always spot them by their wide-eyed curiosity in the Boqueria, and the way they instinctively reach for the plastic-wrapped baguettes.
